mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and driving negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized expansion across durable goods and services demand, which historically precedes broad-based corporate revenue acceleration and positive equity momentum over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production reflects weakening aggregate demand and capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and forces equity multiples lower over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines are consistently followed by positive S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal exhibits no predictive power for forward returns beyond 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly GDP growth directly drives short-term earnings revision momentum, as higher aggregate output validates corporate revenue guidance and sustains positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over a 10-year sample, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during positive GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces real interest rates higher and compresses equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as monetary policy tightens.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65% of high-inflation episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which historically supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor market signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of historical episodes, or the interaction effect disappears when tested against a bootstrap distribution of randomized macro data.

mechanism_narrative: GDP reacceleration following a technical recession initiates a cyclical equity rally as earnings bottoms, forward guidance improves, and risk appetite returns, producing positive returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 turns positive after two consecutive negative quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative or flat in more than 45% of post-recession GDP reacceleration episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 20-year walk-forward test.
